resources.string: No check-in yet today. Pick how you feel below or write in Journal.
How are you feeling right now?
A calm space for your mood and thoughts.
You have not journaled yet today. A few honest lines are enough to start.
There is no wrong way to journal. Short notes count. Your words stay on your account.
Write freely. Your entry is saved privately and analyzed only to suggest a supportive note.
Please keep your entry at or under %1$d words.
Could not save your entry. Please try again.
Each bar uses the last journal entry of that calendar day on this device.
Add a journal entry any day to fill in the chart.
Try a 2-minute breathing pause before you check your phone tomorrow.
Save a journal entry to see your latest mood insight here.

Mindora is not a medical device and does not provide diagnosis or treatment. It is a self-care journaling companion.

If you feel overwhelmed, unsafe, or in crisis, contact a qualified professional or local emergency services.

By continuing, you acknowledge this information.
If you are in immediate danger, contact emergency services in your region.

string-array.name: feedback_happy
string-array.item: It sounds like something lifted you today. Savor small wins—they add up.
Glad you shared this. Keep doing what helps you feel steady and kind to yourself.
Your words carry warmth. Consider one thing you are grateful for before you close the app.

string-array.name: feedback_sad
string-array.item: Thank you for trusting this space. Be gentle with yourself today; even rest is productive.
Heavy days are hard. A short walk, water, or a few slow breaths can be a small step forward.
It is okay to feel low. If it persists or feels too heavy, reaching out to someone you trust can help.

string-array.name: feedback_angry
string-array.item: Anger often signals a boundary or need. When you can, name what felt unfair without judging yourself.
Your feelings are valid. Try a brief pause—inhale four counts, exhale six—before you decide your next step.
It is fine to step away from what is frustrating you. A change of scene can create room to think.

string-array.name: feedback_neutral
string-array.item: Thanks for checking in. A tiny intention for the rest of the day can still matter.
Neutral days are part of the rhythm. Notice one small detail around you that feels okay.
Showing up to reflect is already a healthy habit. Keep going at your own pace.
